payload.text:
allow dragging the frame of the child content to modify the position, allow select the frame to modify the width and height of the child content, the mother content should be named the chapter name.
DO NOT MAKE ANY OTHER CHANGES.

**Refining Mind Map Interactions**

I've been focusing on enhancing the "Mind Map" feature. Recently, I implemented the ability to drag the frame of child content within the mind map to modify its position. This utilizes `nodeOverrides` and SVG `g` transforms. Currently, I am turning my attention to allowing users to select multiple map nodes.


**Implementing Resize & Chapter Names**

I've successfully added resize handles to mind map nodes, allowing adjustment of width and height. I'm ensuring the root node title accurately reflects the active chapter name and am refining the implementation in `components/Notebook.tsx`. I'm verifying the existing drag and resize logic is fully functional, and updating existing UI elements, such as `nodeOverrides`, and `interaction`.


**Verifying Data Structures**

I'm verifying the data structures used for drag and resize operations, specifically `override.dx`, `override.dy`, `override.dw`, and `override.dh`. These are critical for updating the `renderTree` function. I'm focusing on how `handleMapMouseMove` and `handleMapMouseUp` interact to achieve this. Also, I'm checking that `handleInitiateMindMap` correctly sets the label, particularly how it handles the title.


**Confirming Chapter Label Updates**

I'm confirming the root node's label consistently updates to match the chapter title. I've ensured this happens within `handleInitiateMindMap`, and I'm cross-referencing this behavior with `renderTree` to make sure the label display is correct. I am working on how the chapter titles are set, and how this affects the root node.


**Finalizing UI Interactions**

I'm now integrating resize handles into the mind map nodes within `Notebook.tsx`. I've ensured that the bottom-right handle triggers the `startNodeResize` function, allowing adjustments to width and height. This functionality complements the existing drag-and-drop mechanics. I am focusing on the interaction logic to ensure smooth and intuitive operation. I'm also confirming the `generateMindMapStructure` correctly sets the root node label to the chapter name.


**Analyzing AI Integration**

I'm now investigating how to incorporate AI-generated content into the mind map, specifically focusing on the Gemini API. My goal is to structure AI responses as valid JSON, with the book title as the root node. Also, the `pointer-events: none` on the container was blocking interactions. I have modified `Notebook.tsx` to fix this, and now I'm working to make the drag logic work.


**Updating Mouse Interaction**

I've been working on the mouse interaction updates. Specifically, I'm integrating logic to handle pan and drag behaviors. The interaction updates for `drag` and `resize` are now implemented, incorporating delta changes in position and size. This will make the user interaction better, and the UI more responsive. I'm focusing on ensuring these interactions correctly update the mind map node overrides.

entries.description: Enable node dragging and resizing in Mind Map view. Ensure the root node label is strictly set to the chapter name. Improved interaction logic for dragging frames and using resize handles.

**Analyzing Title Display**

I'm focusing on modifying the title display within the `VOICE_SYNTH` player, specifically in the `AudioBook.tsx` component. My goal is to dynamically generate the title as "Chapter Name + Page Number." I'm examining the code within `AudioBook.tsx` to understand the current title rendering and identify where to integrate this change.


**Refining Title Implementation**

I've homed in on the title rendering within the `AudioBook.tsx` component. The current display is "AUDIO_SYNTH: PG.XX", and I'm updating it to incorporate `chapter.title` alongside the page number. I see that the `chapter` prop is accessible, which contains the chapter's title. I intend to change the inner HTML of the `span` to be `{chapter.title} PG.{String(currentPage + 1).padStart(2,'0')}`.


**Implementing Title Refinement**

I'm now zeroing in on the exact code modification needed in `AudioBook.tsx`. The goal is to replace the hardcoded "AUDIO_SYNTH:" prefix with the more relevant `chapter.title`. My analysis indicates I should change line 466 to dynamically incorporate chapter titles. I'll make the title uppercase for stylistic consistency. Specifically, I'm updating the `<span>` element to display the chapter title followed by "PG.XX".


**Updating Title Element**

I'm now focusing on directly updating the JSX within `AudioBook.tsx` to reflect the chapter title. I've pinpointed the exact `<span>` element where the title is rendered. I'm updating it to include the chapter title, converting it to uppercase for a stylistic match with the other text, before appending "PG.XX". I'm also going to check for other title mentions in the component to maintain consistency. This change aims to provide more context to the user within the player overlay.

entries.description: Modified the player title in the Voice Synth view to display the chapter name combined with the current page number for better navigation context.

The goal is to make sure the same icon is consistent throughout, starting with the top controller in `Visualizer.tsx`.
